channel.title: Spring
channel.description: Level up your Java code and explore what Spring can do for you.

item.title: This Week in Spring - May 12th, 2026
item.dc:creator: joshlong
item.content:encoded:
Hi, Spring fans! As I write this I am in Miami, FL at the CodeRemix.ai show, focused on the wide and wonderful world of OpenRewrite and Moderne. I've got a talk to give so let's dive right into it!

  • a quick note about the upcoming release train dates
  • in last week's installment of A Bootiful Podcast, I talked to the legendary Daniel Garnier-Moiroux about his new book Testing Spring Boot Applications
  • I love Craig Walls' Spring AI recipes, in this case building a graph-based agentic workflow!
  • speaking of, Matthew Meckes did a nice talk on Spring AI for GOTO
  • this is interesting: a discussion on database locks and HikariCP and how to scale your systems
  • Eeeenteresting! A very cool Spring Boot-based event sourcing library
  • A nice look at LLM MCP integrations in Java architecture
  • Spring AI 1.0.7, 1.1.6, 2.0.0-M6 available now
  • Spring Cloud Function and Config Have Been Released to Address Several CVEs
  • this looks interesting: a Redis Caching tutorial in terms of Spring Boot

item.title: May Release Train Date Changes
item.dc:creator: mminella
item.content:encoded: In preparation for the May release train which will include updates across the portfolio and new minor versions (Spring Boot 4.1, etc) we are shifting the release train from the originally scheduled timeline from May 11-22 to the new timeline of June 1-5. This applies to all OSS versions including new minor releases and patch releases. https://calendar.spring.io will be updated soon in accordance with this. We look forward to your feedback on the upcoming new features!

item.title: Spring Office Hours Podcast: S5E15 - Upgrading Spring and OSS Security
item.dc:creator: danvega
item.content:encoded:
Join Dan Vega and DaShaun Carter for the latest updates from the Spring Ecosystem. In this episode, Dan and DaShaun tackle two challenges every Spring developer faces: keeping applications up to date and staying ahead of security vulnerabilities in open source dependencies. They explore how AI tools are reshaping the upgrade experience, from automated migration recipes to intelligent vulnerability detection and remediation. Learn what's available today, what's coming next, and how to put these tools to work in your own projects. You can participate in our live stream to ask questions or catch the replay on your preferred podcast platform.

Show Notes

  • Enterprise Support

item.title: Spring Cloud Function and Config Have Been Released To Address Several CVEs
item.dc:creator: ryanjbaxter
item.content:encoded:
On behalf of the Spring Cloud team and everyone who has contributed, I am pleased to announce the following releases of Spring Cloud Function and Spring Cloud Config:

Spring Cloud Config

  • 5.0.3 (OSS)
  • 4.3.3 (OSS)
  • 4.2.7 (Enterprise Only)
  • 4.1.10 (Enterprise Only)
  • 3.1.13 (Enterprise Only)

The above releases address contain fixes for the following CVEs:

  • CVE-2026-40982
  • CVE-2026-40981
  • CVE-2026-41002
  • CVE-2026-41004

Spring Cloud Function

  • 5.0.2 (OSS)
  • 4.3.3 (OSS)
  • 4.1.10 (Enterprise Only)
  • 4.2.6 (Enterprise Only)
  • 3.2.16(Enterprise Only)

The above releases address contain fixes for the following CVEs:

  • CVE-2026-40989
  • CVE-2026-40990

Please refer to the releases page for more details.

Project Page | GitHub | Issues | Documentation
